post.description: Create and download a portable backup archive (.tar) containing wallet.db, exit.db, and a JSON manifest. The response is a binary file stream — save it directly to disk.

Upload a previously exported .tar backup and restore it. Replaces wallet.db and exit.db, then re-syncs WireGuard peers with the restored data. If multihop is active, it is torn down automatically before restore to keep kernel state consistent.

Create a new WireGuard client with the given name. Generates a key pair, allocates IPv4/IPv6 addresses from the pool, and registers the peer on the WireGuard interface. Returns 409 if the name already exists, 400 if the pool is full.

get.description: Return a paginated list of all WireGuard clients. Supports search by name and ascending/descending order by creation date.

post.description: Retrieve the full record for a single client including private key material. Returns 404 if the client does not exist.

Generate a WireGuard configuration file for the given client and IP version. Returns the config as a base64-encoded string. Returns 404 if the client does not exist, 400 if the required endpoint is not configured.
Remove a WireGuard client by name. Deletes the peer from the interface and releases the allocated IP addresses back to the pool. Returns 400 if the client does not exist.

get.description: Return all firewall rule groups. Each group contains a set of nftables rules and routing policies applied as a unit.

post.description: Retrieve a single firewall group by name. Returns 404 if the group does not exist.
Return nftables firewall rules. Optionally filter by group name — omit the group field to list rules from all groups.
Return IP routing and policy rules. Optionally filter by group name — omit the group field to list rules from all groups.

get.description: Return the raw nftables ruleset as JSON (output of 'nft -j list ruleset'). Useful for debugging the live kernel state.
Returns daemon status and version. Use this endpoint to verify that the daemon process is running and reachable.
Return current IPv4/IPv6 subnets, DNS configuration for both address families, and IP address pool utilization (total, assigned, free).

post.description: Change the IPv4 CIDR prefix length. The pool is expanded or contracted and existing clients are migrated to the new subnet. Returns 400 if the new prefix cannot accommodate current clients.

get.description: Run integrity checks on the IP address pool. Returns a list of errors if inconsistencies are found (e.g. duplicate addresses, out-of-range allocations). An empty error list means the pool is healthy.
Return the current WireGuard interface status including public key, listen port, and all registered peers with their live traffic statistics, handshake timestamps, and connection endpoints.

post.description: Look up a single peer by client name and return its live WireGuard status. Returns 404 if the client does not exist or the peer is not registered on the interface.
Return the primary and secondary DNS servers for the given address family (v4 or v6).
Update the primary and secondary DNS servers for the given address family. Returns the updated DNS record. Returns 400 if the provided addresses are invalid.
Import a WireGuard .conf file as a new exit server configuration. Parses the config to extract endpoint, keys, and allowed IPs. Returns 400 if the config is invalid or the name already exists.
Delete an exit server configuration by name. The exit must not be currently active — disable multihop first if needed.

get.description: Return all imported exit server configurations along with the current multihop state (enabled/disabled and active exit name).

post.description: Activate multihop routing through the named exit server. Creates the exit WireGuard interface, applies firewall forwarding rules, and starts the tunnel. Multihop must be disabled first — switching exits requires disable then re-enable. Returns 404 if the exit does not exist.
Deactivate multihop routing. Tears down the exit WireGuard interface and removes the firewall forwarding rules. Client traffic returns to the default direct route.

get.description: Return current multihop state: enabled/disabled, active exit server details, and live WireGuard peer statistics (handshake, RX/TX) for the exit tunnel.

prefix.title: Prefix
prefix.description: New IPv4 CIDR prefix length (16–30). Expanding the prefix migrates existing clients to the new subnet.

ChangeCidrRequest.title: ChangeCidrRequest
ChangeCidrRequest.description: Request body for changing the IPv4 CIDR prefix length.

ChangeCidrResponse.title: ChangeCidrResponse
ChangeCidrResponse.description: Result after a CIDR change including updated subnets and pool stats.

ChangeDnsRequest.title: ChangeDnsRequest
ChangeDnsRequest.description: Request body for updating DNS servers.

ClientNameRequest.title: ClientNameRequest
ClientNameRequest.description: Request body identifying a client by name.

ClientRecord.title: ClientRecord
ClientRecord.description: Full client record including private key material.

ClientSummary.title: ClientSummary
ClientSummary.description: Client summary without private key material (used in list responses).

name.title: Name
name.description: Client name to export config for.

version.title: Version
version.description: IP version: v4 (IPv4-only), v6 (IPv6-only), or hybrid (dual-stack).

ConfigExportRequest.title: ConfigExportRequest
ConfigExportRequest.description: Request body for exporting a client WireGuard configuration.

DnsDetail.title: DnsDetail
DnsDetail.description: Primary and secondary DNS server addresses for one IP family.

DnsFamilyRequest.title: DnsFamilyRequest
DnsFamilyRequest.description: Request body selecting an IP address family.

DnsRecord.title: DnsRecord
DnsRecord.description: DNS server pair for a single address family.

ExitListResponse.title: ExitListResponse
ExitListResponse.description: List of all imported exit servers with current multihop state.

ExitNameRequest.title: ExitNameRequest
ExitNameRequest.description: Request body identifying an exit server by name.

ExitSummary.title: ExitSummary
ExitSummary.description: Summary of an imported exit server configuration.

FirewallRuleRecord.title: FirewallRuleRecord
FirewallRuleRecord.description: A single nftables firewall rule within a group.

GroupFilterRequest.title: GroupFilterRequest
GroupFilterRequest.description: Optional group filter — omit to list all rules.

GroupNameRequest.title: GroupNameRequest
GroupNameRequest.description: Request body identifying a firewall group by name.

GroupRecord.title: GroupRecord
GroupRecord.description: Firewall rule group (e.g. base, nat, multihop preset).

name.title: Name
name.description: Unique name for this exit server.

config.title: Config
config.description: WireGuard .conf file contents (plain text, not base64).

ImportRequest.title: ImportRequest
ImportRequest.description: Request body for importing an exit server configuration.

MultihopStatus.title: MultihopStatus
MultihopStatus.description: Current multihop state including active exit and live peer stats.

NetworkStatus.title: NetworkStatus
NetworkStatus.description: Current network configuration including subnets, DNS, and pool stats.

PeerInfo.title: PeerInfo
PeerInfo.description: Live status of a single WireGuard peer from the kernel interface.

PeerQuery.title: PeerQuery
PeerQuery.description: Request body to look up a peer by client name.

PeerStatus.title: PeerStatus
PeerStatus.description: Live WireGuard peer statistics for the active exit tunnel.

RestoreResult.title: RestoreResult
RestoreResult.description: Summary returned after a successful backup restore.

RoutingRuleRecord.title: RoutingRuleRecord
RoutingRuleRecord.description: An IP routing/policy rule managed by a firewall group.

WireGuardStatus.title: WireGuardStatus
WireGuardStatus.description: Full WireGuard interface status including all peers.
